verses.latin_vulgate: Occasiones quærit qui vult recedere ab amico :  omni tempore erit exprobrabilis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְֽ֭תַאֲוָה יְבַקֵּ֣שׁ נִפְרָ֑ד בְּכׇל־תּ֝וּשִׁיָּ֗ה יִתְגַּלָּֽע׃
verses.king_james: Through desire a man, having separated himself, seeketh and intermeddleth with all wisdom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non recipit stultus verba prudentiæ,  nisi ea dixeris quæ versantur in corde ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־יַחְפֹּ֣ץ כְּ֭סִיל בִּתְבוּנָ֑ה כִּ֝֗י אִֽם־בְּהִתְגַּלּ֥וֹת לִבּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: A fool hath no delight in understanding, but that his heart may discover itself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Impius, cum in profundum venerit peccatorum, contemnit ;  sed sequitur eum ignominia et opprobrium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּֽבוֹא־רָ֭שָׁע בָּ֣א גַם־בּ֑וּז וְֽעִם־קָל֥וֹן חֶרְפָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: When the wicked cometh, then cometh also contempt, and with ignominy reproach.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aqua profunda verba ex ore viri,  et torrens redundans fons sapientiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֣יִם עֲ֭מֻקִּים דִּבְרֵ֣י פִי־אִ֑ישׁ נַ֥חַל נֹ֝בֵ֗עַ מְק֣וֹר חׇכְמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The words of a man’s mouth are as deep waters, and the wellspring of wisdom as a flowing brook.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accipere personam impii non est bonum,  ut declines a veritate judicii.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׂאֵ֣ת פְּנֵי־רָשָׁ֣ע לֹא־ט֑וֹב לְהַטּ֥וֹת צַ֝דִּ֗יק בַּמִּשְׁפָּֽט׃
verses.king_james: It is not good to accept the person of the wicked, to overthrow the righteous in judgment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Labia stulti miscent se rixis,  et os ejus jurgia provocat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׂפְתֵ֣י כְ֭סִיל יָבֹ֣אוּ בְרִ֑יב וּ֝פִ֗יו לְֽמַהֲלֻמ֥וֹת יִקְרָֽא׃
verses.king_james: A fool’s lips enter into contention, and his mouth calleth for strokes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Os stulti contritio ejus,  et labia ipsius ruina animæ ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פִּֽי־כְ֭סִיל מְחִתָּה־ל֑וֹ וּ֝שְׂפָתָ֗יו מוֹקֵ֥שׁ נַפְשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.king_james: A fool’s mouth is his destruction, and his lips are the snare of his soul.

verses.latin_vulgate: Verba bilinguis quasi simplicia,  et ipsa perveniunt usque ad interiora ventris.  Pigrum dejicit timor ;  animæ autem effeminatorum esurient.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּבְרֵ֣י נִ֭רְגָּן כְּמִֽתְלַהֲמִ֑ים וְ֝הֵ֗ם יָרְד֥וּ חַדְרֵי־בָֽטֶן׃
verses.king_james: The words of a talebearer are as wounds, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui mollis et dissolutus est in opere suo  frater est sua opera dissipantis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֭ם מִתְרַפֶּ֣ה בִמְלַאכְתּ֑וֹ אָ֥ח ה֝֗וּא לְבַ֣עַל מַשְׁחִֽית׃
verses.king_james: He also that is slothful in his work is brother to him that is a great waster.

verses.latin_vulgate: Turris fortissima nomen Domini ;  ad ipsum currit justus, et exaltabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִגְדַּל־עֹ֭ז שֵׁ֣ם יְהֹוָ֑ה בּֽוֹ־יָר֖וּץ צַדִּ֣יק וְנִשְׂגָּֽב׃
verses.king_james: The name of the Lord is a strong tower: the righteous runneth into it, and is safe.

verses.latin_vulgate: Substantia divitis urbs roboris ejus,  et quasi murus validus circumdans eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֣וֹן עָ֭שִׁיר קִרְיַ֣ת עֻזּ֑וֹ וּכְחוֹמָ֥ה נִ֝שְׂגָּבָ֗ה בְּמַשְׂכִּתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city, and as an high wall in his own conceit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Antequam conteratur, exaltatur cor hominis,  et antequam glorificetur, humiliatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִפְנֵי־שֶׁ֭בֶר יִגְבַּ֣הּ לֶב־אִ֑ישׁ וְלִפְנֵ֖י כָב֣וֹד עֲנָוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Before destruction the heart of man is haughty, and before honour is humility.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui prius respondet quam audiat,  stultum se esse demonstrat, et confusione dignum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵשִׁ֣יב דָּ֭בָר בְּטֶ֣רֶם יִשְׁמָ֑ע אִוֶּ֥לֶת הִיא־ל֝֗וֹ וּכְלִמָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: He that answereth a matter before he heareth it, it is folly and shame unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Spiritus viri sustentat imbecillitatem suam ;  spiritum vero ad irascendum facilem quis poterit sustinere ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֽוּחַ־אִ֭ישׁ יְכַלְכֵּ֣ל מַחֲלֵ֑הוּ וְר֥וּחַ נְ֝כֵאָ֗ה מִ֣י יִשָּׂאֶֽנָּה׃
verses.king_james: The spirit of a man will sustain his infirmity; but a wounded spirit who can bear?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cor prudens possidebit scientiam,  et auris sapientium quærit doctrinam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֵ֣ב נָ֭בוֹן יִקְנֶה־דָּ֑עַת וְאֹ֥זֶן חֲ֝כָמִ֗ים תְּבַקֶּשׁ־דָּֽעַת׃
verses.king_james: The heart of the prudent getteth knowledge; and the ear of the wise seeketh knowledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Donum hominis dilatat viam ejus,  et ante principes spatium ei facit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַתָּ֣ן אָ֭דָם יַרְחִ֣יב ל֑וֹ וְלִפְנֵ֖י גְדֹלִ֣ים יַנְחֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: A man’s gift maketh room for him, and bringeth him before great men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Justus prior est accusator sui :  venit amicus ejus, et investigabit eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צַדִּ֣יק הָרִאשׁ֣וֹן בְּרִיב֑וֹ (יבא) [וּבָֽא־]רֵ֝עֵ֗הוּ וַחֲקָרֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: He that is first in his own cause seemeth just; but his neighbour cometh and searcheth him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Contradictiones comprimit sors,  et inter potentes quoque dijudicat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֭דְיָנִים יַשְׁבִּ֣ית הַגּוֹרָ֑ל וּבֵ֖ין עֲצוּמִ֣ים יַפְרִֽיד׃
verses.king_james: The lot causeth contentions to cease, and parteth between the mighty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Frater qui adjuvatur a fratre quasi civitas firma,  et judicia quasi vectes urbium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֗ח נִפְשָׁ֥ע מִקִּרְיַת־עֹ֑ז (ומדונים) [וּ֝מִדְיָנִ֗ים] כִּבְרִ֥יחַ אַרְמֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: A brother offended is harder to be won than a strong city: and their contentions are like the bars of a castle.

verses.latin_vulgate: De fructu oris viri replebitur venter ejus,  et genimina labiorum ipsius saturabunt eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִפְּרִ֣י פִי־אִ֭ישׁ תִּשְׂבַּ֣ע בִּטְנ֑וֹ תְּבוּאַ֖ת שְׂפָתָ֣יו יִשְׂבָּֽע׃
verses.king_james: A man’s belly shall be satisfied with the fruit of his mouth; and with the increase of his lips shall he be filled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mors et vita in manu linguæ ;  qui diligunt eam comedent fructus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מָ֣וֶת וְ֭חַיִּים בְּיַד־לָשׁ֑וֹן וְ֝אֹהֲבֶ֗יהָ יֹאכַ֥ל פִּרְיָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: Death and life are in the power of the tongue: and they that love it shall eat the fruit thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui invenit mulierem bonam invenit bonum,  et hauriet jucunditatem a Domino.  Qui expellit mulierem bonam expellit bonum ;  qui autem tenet adulteram stultus est et impius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מָצָ֣א אִ֭שָּׁה מָ֣צָא ט֑וֹב וַיָּ֥פֶק רָ֝צ֗וֹן מֵיְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Whoso findeth a wife findeth a good thing, and obtaineth favour of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum obsecrationibus loquetur pauper,  et dives effabitur rigide.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּחֲנוּנִ֥ים יְדַבֶּר־רָ֑שׁ וְ֝עָשִׁ֗יר יַעֲנֶ֥ה עַזּֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: The poor useth intreaties; but the rich answereth roughly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir amabilis ad societatem  magis amicus erit quam frater.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֣ישׁ רֵ֭עִים לְהִתְרֹעֵ֑עַ וְיֵ֥שׁ אֹ֝הֵ֗ב דָּבֵ֥ק מֵאָֽח׃
verses.king_james: A man that hath friends must shew himself friendly: and there is a friend that sticketh closer than a brother.
